commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=32e6ce407a22977673362012fdff0502e70d28c7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a manager-owned memory_search timeout hook, computes a QMD whole-search deadline, refactors memory_search deadline phases, and threads AbortSignal into QMD and mcporter child-process execution.

PR surface: Source +228, Tests +98. Total +326 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main still wraps memory_search with a fixed 15s deadline while the linked issue reports configured QMD query/rerank taking longer. I did not run a live QMD reranker in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action needs contributor real behavior proof and maintainer reconciliation of the overlapping cancellation surface, not a narrow automated repair on its own.

target.title: fix(memory): honor qmd search timeout for memory_search
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

`memory_search` had a fixed 15s outer deadline, but QMD-backed search can legitimately take longer when local query expansion/reranking is enabled and `memory.qmd.limits.timeoutMs` is raised. The previous fix shape was still wrong because it mapped one QMD per-command timeout onto the whole tool lifecycle.

This patch fixes the mismatch with a QMD-owned whole-search deadline:

- Adds an optional `MemorySearchManager.getSearchTimeoutMs()` hook and implements it in `QmdMemoryManager`.
- Computes the QMD whole-search budget from the sequential QMD lifecycle: multi-collection command attempts, unsupported-mode fallback, missing-collection repair retry, pending-update wait, dirty search sync, and timer grace.
- Keeps runtime loading, manager setup, wiki supplements, cooldown supplement paths, and non-QMD/default memory paths on the existing 15s `memory_search` guard.
- Threads abort signals into QMD/mcporter child-process execution so a fired whole-search deadline kills the subprocess tree instead of leaving orphaned work.

Fixes #91947.

## Evidence

Local validation on this branch after the fix:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts` -> 19 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts` -> 130 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts` -> 20 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.test.ts` -> 16 passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory-tool-manager.test-mocks.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/types.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.ts` -> passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/memory-core/src/tools.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory-tool-manager.test-mocks.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/search-manager.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/types.ts packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/qmd-process.ts` -> passed
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` -> passed
- `git diff --check` -> passed

The new regression coverage proves two review-critical behaviors: QMD manager setup still times out at 15s, while an actual QMD-backed memory search can complete after the old 15s deadline when the manager exposes a larger whole-search budget.
